articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: New financing model targets 5,000 smallholder farmers
more.description: Source: New financing model targets 5,000 smallholder farmers -Newsday Zimbabwe HARARE, Apr. 24 (NewsDay Live) – A structured smallholder financing programme targeting rural farmers has been launched, in a move expected to accelerate sector growth and improve market access. more.title: High fuel prices threatening agric output warns ZFU
more.description: Source: High fuel prices threatening agric output warns ZFU –Newsday Zimbabwe HARARE, Apr. 24 (NewsDay) – The Zimbabwe Farmers Union (ZFU) has raised alarm over rising fuel prices, warning that recent increases are beginning to undermine agricultural productivity, particularly for winter wheat farmers currently in the middle of the planting season. more.title: Beating the Market in Zimbabwe: Why Marketing and Sales Capabilities Are the New Competitive Edge
more.description: IN Zimbabwe’s volatile and often unforgiving business environment, consistent growth is difficult. Sustained outperformance is even rarer. Yet a growing body of evidence suggests that the difference between companies that merely survive and those that lead the market lies not in capital investment or scale alone, but in the strength of their marketing and sales [...] The post Beating the Market in Zimbabwe: Why Marketing and Sales Capabilities Are the New Competitive Edge appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Marketing Return on Investment in Zimbabwe: From Spend to Strategy in a Constrained Economy
more.description: IN Zimbabwe’s fragile and highly competitive economic environment, marketing is no longer judged by visibility alone. It is increasingly evaluated by one question: what does it deliver? By Our Insights Team With constrained budgets, currency volatility, and intensifying competition across sectors, businesses are under pressure to ensure that every dollar spent on marketing translates into [...] The post Marketing Return on Investment in Zimbabwe: From Spend to Strategy in a Constrained Economy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: From Data Scarcity to Data Sovereignty: How Zimbabwean Business Can Leapfrog into the AI Age
more.description: IN boardrooms across Harare, Bulawayo, and Victoria Falls, a quiet but consequential shift is underway. Decisions that were once shaped by instinct, experience, and fragmented reports are increasingly being driven by data—structured, unstructured, real-time, and predictive. What was once unknowable is now discoverable within minutes. more.title: Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe
more.description: Zimbabwe stands at an unusual intersection of necessity and opportunity. On one hand, the pressures are unmistakable—energy insecurity, climate variability, industrial stagnation, and constrained capital. On the other, the country possesses the raw ingredients of a green transition: abundant sunlight, agricultural depth, mineral wealth, and a youthful population increasingly attuned to technology and sustainability. The […]The post Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: AI as the New Industrial Engine: How Zimbabwean Businesses Can Build Faster, Smarter, and Globally Competitive Ventures
more.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer a distant technological promise—it is rapidly becoming the foundation upon which modern businesses are built. Across the world, companies are using AI to compress development timelines, reduce operating costs, and scale at unprecedented speed. For Zimbabwe, where businesses operate within tight financial constraints and structural inefficiencies, AI presents more than […]The post AI as the New Industrial Engine: How Zimbabwean Businesses Can Build Faster, Smarter, and Globally Competitive Ventures appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Land Incentives Framework Sparks Political Storm as Critics Accuse Kudakwashe Tagwirei of Overreach
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe’s newly announced land purchase incentives framework—positioned by the government as a tool to broaden access to land and support agricultural productivity—has triggered a wave of backlash from opposition figures, former ruling party insiders, and governance watchdogs, who allege abuse of office, lack of transparency, and creeping state capture. more.title: Chatunga Mugabe Shooting Case: Victim Allegedly Paid R250,000 With Further R150,000 Promised
more.description: JOHANNESBURG — New details have emerged in the high-profile shooting case involving Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, with a South African court hearing that the victim was allegedly paid R250,000 in cash and promised an additional R150,000 following the incident.
